notes: Each PNG must remain at the exact PixelLab output dimensions.
Cropping, padding, resampling, and runtime replacement are outside this candidate run.
Native dimensions and alpha bounds are recorded after the asynchronous jobs complete.
PixelLab's completed status text reported each rail as 48x48, but both the inline PNG payloads and directly downloaded PNG headers are 48x32. Validation uses the PNG bytes.
The three v2 entries are the single permitted native reroll for first-pass footprint misses; no corrective resize or third reroll was performed.

candidates.prompt: Native-resolution pixel-art game asset: one isolated low rustic wooden picket-fence rail segment, without terminal posts. Two rough horizontal oak rails connect five narrow, slightly uneven vertical pickets; warm medium-brown wood with a few dark grain marks and worn cut ends. Cozy handcrafted fantasy tactical-board style, crisp chunky pixels, limited palette, selective dark-brown outline, basic readable shading. Low top-down isometric three-quarter view, one straight segment running diagonally from lower-left to upper-right. Fill roughly 46 by 28 pixels while remaining completely inside the exact 48 by 32 canvas with a transparent margin. Fence only: no terrain, grass, flowers, gate, sign, loose debris, cast shadow, glow, text, or watermark.

Keep every pixel within y=0..31. The vertical wooden pickets should be approximately 16 pixels tall from their local bottom to tip, connected by two rough horizontal oak rails. Use six narrow slightly uneven pickets, warm medium-brown wood, restrained grain, limited palette, crisp chunky native pixels, selective dark-brown outline, basic readable shading. Low top-down isometric three-quarter view, straight segment angled only gently from lower-left to upper-right so the 16-pixel upright height fits the 32-pixel canvas. Fence only: no endpoint pillars, terrain, grass, flowers, gate, sign, debris, cast shadow, glow, text, or watermark. Do not draw a border or frame.

Native-resolution pixel-art game asset on an EXACT 32 by 32 pixel transparent canvas. Draw exactly one isolated terminal post for a low rustic wooden picket fence. No antialiasing, glow, shadow, or stray semitransparent pixels outside that box. The post is a slim square warm-brown oak timber with slightly hand-hewn edges, a compact bevelled cap that remains within the same 10-pixel width, two subtle dark rail-attachment notches, and restrained grain. Cozy handcrafted fantasy tactical-board style, crisp chunky native pixels, limited palette, selective dark-brown outline, basic readable shading, low top-down isometric three-quarter view.
